title: ARINC 811 / DO-326A network-domain segregation drift detection - any unmediated information flow from PIESD/POD to AISD/ACD on the in-service fleet
exclusions: Excludes Class 1 EFB devices that are personally owned and physically isolated from aircraft systems. Excludes aircraft not equipped with networked PIESD (e.g. regional turboprops without IFE or passenger Wi-Fi).
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.60.3` archived hourly to `audit_evidence` with `do326a_clause="DO-326A-s4-1"`. Every row pins (tail_number, airframe_type, observation_time, source_domain, destination_domain, flow_direction, mediator_device, packet_count, bytes, severity, risk_score).

compliance.assurance_rationale: DO-326A §4.1 requires that information flow from lower-trust to higher-trust ARINC 811 domains be either prohibited or mediated by an evaluated security boundary device. This UC continuously verifies that the in-service fleet honours the type-certificate security architecture.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates that no unmediated PIESD-to-AISD or POD-to-ACD information flow exists on the operating fleet; any observation triggers immediate investigation and (if confirmed) aircraft-from-service isolation pending root-cause analysis.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search archived to `audit_evidence`, the `DO-326A ARINC 811 Domain Segregation` dashboard, the security boundary device (network guard / data diode / controlled-interface device) configuration baselines, the wireshark / OT-sensor packet captures.

compliance.assurance_rationale: ARINC 811 §4.2 defines the four aircraft information security domains; this UC monitors the inter-domain boundary that the standard establishes.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidences continuous monitoring of the ARINC 811 domain boundary integrity on the in-service fleet.

compliance.assurance_rationale: EASA Part-IS IS.OR.220 requires detection, response, and recovery from information security incidents that could affect aviation safety; an unmediated lower-to-higher-trust flow is a textbook IS.OR.220 incident.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidences detection capability against information-security incidents at the aircraft information-security domain boundary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: DO-326A §2.1 sets the top-level airworthiness security objective: prevent intentional unauthorized electronic interaction with aircraft safety. An unmediated PIESD-to-ACD or POD-to-ACD flow is the canonical unauthorized electronic interaction with the safety-of-flight domain; this UC operationalises detection of exactly that condition on the in-service fleet.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates that the §2.1 objective is enforced operationally and not only at design time, by alerting whenever an unauthorized electronic interaction crosses into the safety-of-flight domain.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Inject a synthetic packet from the PIESD test rig with a destination IP in the AISD address range; do not route it through the controlled-interface device. UC-22.60.3 MUST flag within 5 minutes with risk_score >= 90 and trigger the runbook.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Send a permitted higher-to-lower information flow (ACD telemetry broadcast to AISD ACMS) through the approved data diode. UC-22.60.3 MUST NOT flag, because the source_domain (ACD) is higher-trust than the destination_domain (AISD) and the mediator_device is the approved data diode.

dataSources: Cisco Cyber Vision OT flow events (`cisco:cybervision:flow`), Claroty xDome inter-zone events (`claroty:xdome:alert`), Nozomi Guardian aircraft network events (`nozomi:guardian:event`), Aircraft Network Switch port-mirror traffic via stream (sourcetype `aero:network:flow`), Aircraft Interface Device (AID) audit (`aid:audit`), Wireless Onboard LAN Unit (WOLU) audit, ARINC 811 domain mapping table (KV Store `arinc811_domain_lookup` mapping IP / VLAN / MAC to ARINC 811 domain), security boundary device configuration baselines.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for Cisco Cyber Vision (Splunkbase 6071), Splunk Add-on for Claroty (Splunkbase 5018), Splunk Add-on for Nozomi Networks (Splunkbase 4894), Splunk_TA_stream (Splunkbase 1809), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).

splunkbaseApps.name: Splunk Add-on for Cisco Cyber Vision

spl:
(index=aero_network sourcetype IN ("cisco:cybervision:flow", "claroty:xdome:alert", "nozomi:guardian:event", "aero:network:flow"))
| lookup arinc811_domain_lookup src_ip OUTPUT arinc811_domain AS source_domain
| lookup arinc811_domain_lookup dest_ip OUTPUT arinc811_domain AS destination_domain
| eval trust_rank_src = case(source_domain = "ACD", 4, source_domain = "AISD", 3, source_domain = "PIESD", 2, source_domain = "POD", 1, 1=1, 0)
| eval trust_rank_dst = case(destination_domain = "ACD", 4, destination_domain = "AISD", 3, destination_domain = "PIESD", 2, destination_domain = "POD", 1, 1=1, 0)
| eval flow_direction = case(trust_rank_src > trust_rank_dst, "higher-to-lower", trust_rank_src < trust_rank_dst, "lower-to-higher", 1=1, "intra-domain")
| where flow_direction = "lower-to-higher"
| eval mediator_device = coalesce(mediator_device, security_boundary_device, "none")
| eval risk_score = case(
    destination_domain = "ACD" AND mediator_device = "none", 100,
    destination_domain = "AISD" AND mediator_device = "none", 90,
    destination_domain = "ACD" AND mediator_device != "approved", 95,
    destination_domain = "AISD" AND mediator_device != "approved", 80,
    1=1, 0)
| where risk_score > 0
| stats count AS packet_count sum(bytes) AS bytes earliest(_time) AS first_seen latest(_time) AS last_seen values(src_ip) AS src_ips values(dest_ip) AS dest_ips by tail_number airframe_type source_domain destination_domain flow_direction mediator_device risk_score
| eval severity = case(risk_score >= 95, "critical", risk_score >= 80, "high", 1=1, "medium")
| sort 0 -risk_score, -packet_count
| table tail_number airframe_type first_seen last_seen source_domain destination_domain flow_direction mediator_device packet_count bytes src_ips dest_ips severity risk_score
description: Continuously scans Cisco Cyber Vision / Claroty xDome / Nozomi Guardian flow events and aircraft-network port-mirror traffic for any information flow from a lower-trust ARINC 811 domain (POD or PIESD) into a higher-trust domain (AISD or ACD) that is not mediated by an approved security boundary device. The DO-326A type-certificate finding is that no such flow exists.
value: An unmediated PIESD-to-ACD flow is one of the highest-impact airworthiness findings imaginable - it is the threat scenario that prompted the FAA's Special Conditions for the Boeing 787 in 2007 and that the Chris Roberts 2015 incident was investigated against. A confirmed flow may require the operator to immediately ground the aircraft, the TC-holder to issue an Emergency Airworthiness Directive, and the regulator to open a public investigation. This UC's value is to catch the drift before it becomes a safety event.
implementation: (1) Populate `arinc811_domain_lookup` with IP / VLAN / MAC ranges per ARINC 811 domain per airframe type, sourced from the TC-holder Security Architecture Description (SAD) - typically `Aircraft Control Domain = 10.0.0.0/16 on VLAN 100`, `AISD = 10.1.0.0/16 on VLAN 200`, `PIESD = 10.2.0.0/16 on VLAN 300`, `POD = 10.3.0.0/16 on VLAN 400`. (2) Tap aircraft network switches (Honeywell Stage, Collins Aerospace, Astronics) into a Splunk Edge Hub / OT collector. (3) Schedule UC-22.60.3 every 5 minutes; route `risk_score >= 90` at severity P1, `risk_score 60-89` at severity P2. (4) Build the `DO-326A ARINC 811 Domain Segregation` dashboard.
visualization: (Panel 1) Critical flows by destination domain (ACD vs AISD). (Panel 2) Flow heatmap (tail x time-of-day). (Panel 3) Flow source/dest matrix (Sankey diagram). (Panel 4) 30-day flow violation trend with response time.

references.title: RTCA DO-326A - Airworthiness Security Process Specification
ARINC 811 - Commercial Aircraft Information Security Concepts of Operation and Process Framework
FAA Special Conditions for Boeing 787 - Aircraft Electronic System Security

knownFalsePositives:
1. **Permitted flow without mediator metadata**: a permitted PIESD-to-AISD flow (e.g. EFB host pulling cabin-PA announcement schedule from IFE content server) requires the mediator_device to be tagged in the flow; if the flow is enriched correctly the flow won't fire. 2. **Mis-classified ARINC 811 mapping**: a misconfiguration in `arinc811_domain_lookup` can cause false positives; baseline `arinc811_domain_lookup` against TC-holder SAD at every release. 3. **Test-bench traffic**: integration-test rigs may generate traffic that mimics aircraft flows; tag test rigs with `test_environment=true` and exclude.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `do326a_22_60_3_exceptions` with the Chief Engineer + CISO documented approval, an explicit expiry date, and a written justification.
reviewer: Aviation Cybersecurity SME Panel + Chief Engineer + Network Architect + CISO
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- UC-22.60.1 and UC-22.60.2 in production for the airframe types in scope.
- KV Store `arinc811_domain_lookup` populated from TC-holder Security Architecture Description.
- Aircraft-network port-mirror capability into a Splunk Edge Hub or OT sensor.
- Cisco Cyber Vision / Claroty xDome / Nozomi Guardian (one or more) feeding `cisco:cybervision:flow` / `claroty:xdome:alert` / `nozomi:guardian:event`.

## Step 1 - ARINC 811 domain mapping

```ini
[arinc811_domain_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.airframe_type = string
field.ip_range = string
field.vlan = string
field.mac_prefix = string
field.arinc811_domain = string
field.security_boundary_device = string
field.security_boundary_device_status = string
```

## Step 2 - Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.60.3: DO-326A ARINC 811 Segregation]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -10m@m
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable, soar
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.60.3,reg=DO-326A,clause=DO-326A-s4-1
alert.severity = 5
```

## Step 3 - Runbooks

**`risk_score >= 95`** (owner: Chief Engineer + CISO + Director of Flight Operations, severity P1): Within 30 minutes, the operator's Operations Control Centre (OCC) initiates an aircraft-from-service isolation decision; within 2 hours, the TC-holder is notified; within 24 hours, EASA / FAA are notified per Part-IS IS.OR.230 / FAA reporting framework.

**`risk_score 60-94`** (owner: CISO + Network Architect, severity P2): Within 4 hours, the security boundary device is verified to be present and operating per its baseline; if not, escalate to P1.

## Step 4 - Aviation-specific notes

- **OEM exemption flows**: some OEM remote-monitoring channels traverse the ARINC 811 boundary via TC-holder-approved paths; these are tagged with `mediator_device=approved` so they will not fire the alert.
- **Type-certificate scope**: this UC monitors a finding under the type-certificate; the operator must coordinate with the TC-holder on any architectural deviation.
- **Older fleet (no AISD)**: aircraft delivered before AISD became standard (early-1990s) may have a simpler architecture - tag with `arinc811_legacy=true` and tune the rule.
grandmaExplanation: An aircraft has separate computer networks: one for flying the plane, one for the airline staff, and one for the passenger Wi-Fi. We watch that no information crosses from the passenger Wi-Fi side into the flying-the-plane side. If we ever see this, we ground the aircraft right away.
